title: Lissochilus galbanus
intro: Lissochilus galbanus, commonly known as Galbanum, is a perennial plant belonging to the family Apiaceae, native to the mountainous regions of Central Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic activities, making it useful in treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Galbanum include sesquiterpenes, such as galbanic acid and galbanol, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. In traditional medicine, Galbanum is commonly used in herbal preparations such as essential oils, infusions, and tinctures to alleviate symptoms of colds, coughs, and bronchitis.
